horizon-1-core.title: Horizon 1 — Core business
horizon-1-core.prompt: List the current businesses that fund the company today. Capture how each should be defended, improved, or extended.
examples: Insurance and banking core generating cash
Mature subscription product with high retention
Flagship product line facing gradual disruption

horizon-2-emerging.title: Horizon 2 — Emerging growth
horizon-2-emerging.prompt: List businesses that already show evidence and may become material growth engines if scaled well.
examples: Digital health platform with early traction
Enterprise AI workflow product moving from pilots to paid teams
New geography with repeatable unit economics

horizon-3-options.title: Horizon 3 — Future options
horizon-3-options.prompt: List early options, technologies, market spaces, or business-model ideas that need learning before scale.
examples: New customer segment not yet validated
Technology bet with unclear adoption timing

migration-actions.title: Migration actions
migration-actions.prompt: What must happen for an item to move from H3 to H2, or from H2 to H1? Name funding, governance, transfer, and scaling actions.
examples: Move health platform from venture board to operating unit
Fund the second market expansion after retention evidence
Retire H3 option if regulatory trigger does not appear

evidence-risks.title: Evidence and risks
evidence-risks.prompt: Record the evidence, assumptions, and risks that determine whether each horizon item deserves more funding or a different action.
examples: H2 still lacks repeatable acquisition channel
H3 depends on cost curve falling below threshold
H1 moat weakening because new entrant owns customer data
